properties.name: Advice
properties.location: campers along the Goulburn River from Goulburn Weir to Shepparton
properties.webBody:
This Flood Advice is being issued for campers along the edge of the Goulburn River from Goulburn Weir to Shepparton

Goulburn Murray Water is releasing water from the Goulburn Weir, which will result in the river continuing to rise quickly. 
However, river levels with the planned releases should stay within the river channel and below the minor flood level. 

Campers and visitors close to the river should relocate to higher banks away from the river's edge and closely monitor conditions over the next few days. 

Low lying access tracks to the river and camping areas may flood and affect moving around the area.

Riverine flooding of roads, towns and homes is not expected during this warning period. The situation is being monitored closely and further warnings will be issued if conditions change. 
Stay informed - monitor local conditions and remain alert to the river rising quickly overnight. 
What you should do
  • If you are camped within the Goulburn River channel, move your camp away from the River's edge and remain alert to the river continuing to rise, and could rise quickly. 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes. 
  • Monitor weather forecasts and river levels. Go to www.bom.gov.au/vic/warnings/ 
Impacts in your area
  • Low-lying camping spots along on the Goulburn River may become inundated or isolated as the river continues to rise today.
  • Never drive, ride, walk or play in floodwater.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service 

The next update is expected by 02/07/2026 10:00 am or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au 
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app 
  • Facebook or Twitter (Vicfloods) 
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.   
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

This Flood Watch is being re-issued for the King Parrot Creek.

Minor to Moderate flooding likely in the flood watch area from Wednesday
  • Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused river and creek level rises in the Goulburn River catchment. 
  • Widespread rainfall is forecast to continue through to Friday, which is likely to result in further and renewed rises and areas of flooding across the Goulburn River catchment.
  • The situation is being closely monitored, and Flood Warnings will be issued as required.
A Flood Watch is still current for many other parts of North East Victoria.

King Parrot Creek:
River levels are elevated along the King Parrot Creek.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do:
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Decide what you and your family will do if flooding impacts you. For information on how to prepare go to www.ses.vic.gov.au/get-ready/floodsafe.

properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Kiewa River downstream of Mongans Bridge

Moderate flooding possible at Bandiana Thursday morning 
Minor flooding occurring at Kiewa

  • Since Monday, moderate rainfall has been recorded across the Kiewa River catchment, resulting in river level rises. 
  • Further rainfall is forecast for the remainder of the week. 
  • This rainfall may cause further river level rises along the Kiewa River for the next few days, with Moderate flooding possible at Bandiana Thursday morning and Minor flooding continuing at Kiewa over the next few days.
  • A Flood Watch is still current for many other parts of North East Victoria.

Kiewa River downstream of Mongans Bridge:
  • Minor flooding is occurring at Bandiana and Kiewa. 
  • Moderate flooding is possible at Bandiana.

The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa 
  • The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa is currently at 3.34 m and steady, with minor flooding. 
  • The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa is expected to remain around the minor flood level (3.30 m) over the next few days. Further river level rises are possible with forecast rainfall

The Kiewa River at Bandiana 
  • The Kiewa River at Bandiana is currently at 2.96 m and steady, with minor flooding. 
  • The Kiewa River at Bandiana may exceed the moderate flood level (3.10 m) Thursday morning. Further river level rises are possible with forecast rainfall.

Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground if still safe to do so.
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Contact family members and neighbours to ensure they are aware of the situation.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Low lying local roads close to the river may be closed and low bridges may be underwater.
  • Low lying camping areas close to the river may be flooded.
  • Heavy rainfall may lead to an increased risk of landslides, especially in bushfire affetced areas – be aware of fallen earth, rocks, debris or trees.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 02/07/2026 12:30 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.

  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is for the Seven Creeks to Euroa and updates the Flood Watch issued earlier today at  7.54 am.

Minor Flooding is occuring at Euroa and possibly at Kialla West Friday afteroon. 
  • Since Monday, moderate rainfall has been recorded across the Seven Creeks catchment, resulting in river level rises. 
  • A minor flood peak was observed at Strathbogie and minor flooding is occurring at Euroa. 
  • Further rainfall is forecast for the remainder of the week, which may contribute to minor flooding at Kialla West Friday afternoon.
A Flood Watch is still current for many parts of North East Victoria.

Seven Creeks to Euroa
  • Minor flooding is occurring along the Seven Creeks at Euroa.
  • The Seven Creeks at Euroa is currently at 3.36 m and steady, with minor flooding. 
  • The Seven Creeks at Euroa may peak near 3.40 m Wednesday afternoon, with minor flooding. The creek is likely to remain above the minor flood level (2.50 m) for at least the next few days. 
  • Further rises are possible with forecast rainfall.
Seven Creeks downstream of Euroa
  • Minor flooding is possible at Kialla West.
  • The Seven Creeks at Kialla West is currently at 1.73 m and steady, below the minor flood level. 
  • The Seven Creeks at Kialla West may exceed the minor flood level (4.50 m) Friday afternoon. Further rises are possible with forecast rainfall.

Castle Creek
  • Minor flooding is possible at Telfords Bridge Friday evening.


Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground. 
  • Download your Local Flood Guide to understand local flood impacts. www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides/strathbogie-shire-council
  • Review your emergency plan and check your emergency kit is fully stocked, if you have one. 
  • Monitor weather forecasts and river levels. Go to www.bom.gov.au/vic/warnings/
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Heavy rain over fire affected areas increases the risk of landslides. 
  • At the minor flood level of 2.5 metres on the Seven Creeks at Euroa, the Euroa Caravan Park, Rotary Park, and park area around the Memorial Oval and Bowls Club may start to be inundated with water.  
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 02/07/2026 03:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:

  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226

properties.webBody:
This Moderate Flood Warning is being issued for the King River at Docker Road Bridge and a Minor flood Warning for the King River downstream of Lake William Hovell.

Moderate flooding is occurring on the King River at Docker Road bridge.
Minor flooding occurring downstream of Lake William Hovell
  • Moderate rainfall across the Ovens and King River catchments since Monday has caused significant river level rises along the King River, with further rainfall forecast for the remainder of Wednesday and through to Friday.
  • Moderate flooding is occurring at Docker Road Bridge and minor flooding is occurring at downstream of Lake William Hovell. 

The King River
  • Along the King River, minor flooding is occurring at Downstream of Lake William Hovell and Cheshunt. Moderate flooding is occurring at Docker Rd Bridge. 
  • No observations are currently available for the The King River at Lake William Hovell (Downstream). The river level was at 1.89 m and steady, with minor flooding on Wednesday 1st July at 10 am. The King River at Lake William Hovell (Downstream) is likely to remain above the minor flood level (1.80 m) overnight Wednesday into Thursday. Further rises are possible with forecast rain.
  • The King River at Docker Rd Bridge is currently at 3.97 m and steady, with moderate flooding. The King River at Docker Rd Bridge is likely to remain above the moderate flood level (3.95 m) overnight Wednesday into Thursday.
This is an update, replacing the Watch and Act message issued at 6.28pm 1 July 2026 
Prepare for flooding now. Take actions to protect your life and property against potential hazards caused by flooding.
What you should do:
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater.
  • Farmers should move livestock and machinery to higher ground if still safe to do so.
  • Rising floodwater can be dangerous. Be aware of water levels rising near you.
If you choose to leave:
  • Remember to take your pets, mobile phone, spare clothes and medications.
  • Travel to the home of family or friends who are in a safe location, away from flooding.
  • Turn off gas and electricity at your home or workplace.
  • Be aware of any road closures when you leave.
If you are travelling:
  • Do not enter flooded areas. Turn around a find a safe alternative route.
  • Be aware of road hazards including mud, debris and damaged roads or bridges.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
If you stay or if it is unsafe to leave:
  • Make sure you have enough food, drinking water, medications and pet food to survive for three to five days in case you become isolated.
  • Use plugs, sandbags or household items to block toilets, sinks and drains to stop sewerage backflow.
  • Lift it - move valuables and vital household items up on high surfaces, such as on top of tables, chairs, benches and beds.
Impacts in your area:
  • Rural farmland flooding along the river floodplain starts.
  • At Docker Road Bridge, no homes should experience overfloor flooding at this flood height.
  • For more detailed information check the Wangaratta Local Flood Guide https://www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides/rural-city-of-wangaratta#wangaratta-local-flood-guide
At Moderate flood level.   
  • Low lying farmland impacted. 
  • A small number of properties near the Docker Road bridge are likely to become isolated.
Road Closures
  • In the 2022 flood at this flood level, Docker - Carboor Rd was closed between Wangaratta-Whitfield Rd and Oxley-Meadow Creek Rd. 
  • Check the VicTraffic website (www.traffic.transport.vic.gov.au) for road closures and follow instructions from emergency services.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

Minor to Moderate flooding is likely in the Flood Watch areas from Wednesday, with isolated Major flooding possible.

  • Further widespread moderate rainfall is forecast across northeast Victoria from Wednesday into Friday. 
  • This rainfall may result in minor to moderate flooding across the Flood Watch area from Wednesday, with isolated major flooding possible. 
  • Further flooding is likely in the Kiewa River, Ovens and King Rivers, Goulburn River, and Seven Creeks catchments, where Flood Warnings remain current. 
  • The situation is being closely monitored, and Flood Warnings will be issued as required.

properties.webBody:
This Flood Watch is being issued for Campaspe River.

  • In the 48 hours to 9am Wednesday, significant rainfall was observed in the Campaspe catchment, including 68.4mm at Redesdale and 56.2mm at Heathcote.
  • As of 11am Wednesday, Lake Eppalock was 43.0% full, with negligible downstream flows into the Campaspe River. 
  • Isolated areas including downstream of Lake Eppalock saw slightly higher totals. Overland flow is contributing to downstream rises.
  • The Campaspe River at Rochester Town is at 110.60m AHD as of 1pm Wednesday and is not currently likely to reach Minor flood level (113.00m AHD).
  • Should further significant rain fall in the catchment on Wednesday evening and Thursday morning, it is possible that the Campaspe River at Rochester Town could reach the Minor flood level by late Thursday afternoon.
  • Further rises above Minor flood level are not currently predicted.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions.
What you should do:
  • Stay away from flooded drains, rivers, streams and waterways.
  • Obey road closure signs. Plan ahead so you don't drive on flooded roads.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings. For information on how to prepare go to www.ses.vic.gov.au/get-ready/floodsafe.
Impacts in your area: 
  • Rapid rainfall associated with thunderstorms may make driving dangerous. Emergency services have responded to several water rescues in the past 24 hours. 
  • Do not enter floodwater, and do not attempt to pass roads or bridges over flooded waterways.
  • Obey local road closures.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for Seven Creeks downstream of Euroa.

MINOR FLOODING OCCURRING AT EUROA AND POSSIBLE AT KIALLA WEST FRIDAY AFTERNOON
Since Monday, moderate rainfall has been recorded across the Seven Creeks catchment, resulting in river level rises. Minor flooding is occurring at Euroa following a minor flood peak during Wednesday. Upstream at Strathbogie renewed minor flooding is occurring. As these floodwaters move downstream minor flooding may be extended at Euroa. Rainfall is forecast for the remainder of the week, which may result in further river level rises. A Flood Watch is current for Parts of North East and Central Victoria. 

Seven Creeks to Euroa: 
  • Minor flooding is occurring along the Seven Creeks at Strathbogie and Euroa. 
  • The Seven Creeks at Euroa peaked at 3.37 m during Wednesday, with minor flooding. The creek is currently at 2.99 m and falling, with minor flooding. The Seven Creeks at Euroa is likely to remain above the minor flood level (2.50 m) Thursday morning. 
Seven Creeks downstream of Euroa: 
  • Minor flooding is possible at Kialla West. 
  • The Seven Creeks at Kialla West is currently at 1.87 m and steady, below the minor flood level. The Seven Creeks at Kialla West may exceed the minor flood level (4.50 m) Friday afternoon. Further rises are possible with forecast rainfall.
This is an update, replacing the Advice message issued at 4.19pm 1 July 2026
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings. 
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Download Shepparton Mooroopna and Kialla Local Flood Guide to understand local flood impacts. 
  • Review your emergency plan and check your emergency kit is fully stocked, if you have one.
  • Monitor weather forecasts and river levels. Go to www.bom.gov.au/vic/warnings/
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Local roads may be closed and low bridges may be underwater.
  • If Minor flooding does happen in Kialla West:  Mitchell Road may flood between the Goulburn Valley Highway and Archer Street.    
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 02/07/2026 03:00 pm or as the situation changes.
